title: Whitespace before parenthesis or bracket
rationale: PEP 8 states that whitespace should be avoided immediately before the open parenthesis that starts the argument list of a function call, and immediately before the open bracket that starts an indexing or slicing operation.

title: Missing whitespace around operator
rationale: PEP 8 recommends surrounding binary operators with a single space on either side to improve readability, with the exception of using no spaces around the highest priority operator when arguments are simple.

title: Missing whitespace after comma
rationale: PEP 8 specifies that commas, semicolons, and colons should always be followed by whitespace unless followed by the end of a line or a closing bracket.
good_example:
def f(a, b, c):
    return [a, b, c]

title: Expected blank lines between functions and classes
rationale: PEP 8 specifies surrounding top-level function and class definitions with two blank lines, and method definitions inside a class with one blank line, to visually separate logical units of code.

title: Line too long
rationale: PEP 8 limits all lines to a maximum of 79 characters for code, since long lines reduce readability on smaller screens and in side-by-side diffs.
bad_example: result = some_function(argument_one, argument_two, argument_three, argument_four, argument_five)
good_example:
result = some_function(
    argument_one, argument_two, argument_three,
    argument_four, argument_five,
)

title: Comparison to None should use 'is'
rationale: PEP 8 states that comparisons to singletons like None should always be done with 'is' or 'is not', never with the equality operators, because identity comparison is unambiguous and faster.

title: Function names should be lowercase
rationale: PEP 8 specifies that function and variable names should be lowercase, with words separated by underscores as necessary to improve readability, following the snake_case convention.

title: Class names should use CapWords
rationale: PEP 8 specifies that class names should normally use the CapWords (PascalCase) convention to visually distinguish classes from functions and variables.

title: Indentation contains mixed spaces and tabs
rationale: PEP 8 mandates using 4 spaces per indentation level and explicitly states that spaces are the preferred indentation method, with tabs reserved only for maintaining consistency with code already indented with tabs.
